title: My System Combines Algo, Data, and AI to Create Win Rates over 90%
author: jabberw0ckee
selftext:
I built a system that transforms you into a successful trader!

And, AI plays a substantial role.

The AI is amazing because it serves multiple purposes:

1. It has access to all of the systems alerts, data, and statistics so it can calculate the probability of outcomes for all of the alerts the system generates
2. It helps traders with risk management, emotions, and strategy
3. It provides general market sentiment based on current financial, economic and geopolitical news

The foundation is a high win rate algorithmic system with multiple channels. The core strategy is RSI based alerts for either oversold events or mid range reversals. There are three channels with the following raw win rates: 89%, 84%, and 74%.

The Momentum Effect is an important part of the strategy. The Momentum Effect states that stocks that outperform in 6 to 12 months will almost always continue to outperform for the next 1 to 3 months. The system picks the absolute best performers in the market with good market cap and volume. The stocks are updated every 2 weeks to keep the performance window fresh and moving forward with the market.

The alerts are provided to everyone for free along with the AI trade advisor who coaches new traders on how to trade - risk management, sizing, and which alerts have the highest probability of success.

https://preview.redd.it/8wla96qnwj0h1.png?width=2334&format=png&auto=webp&s=6111e1423d2041f5bd2a47f43b181d12794c457c

Instead of just alerting when the condition is met, the system also embeds each alert with all the information that the best traders use to make a decisions about when to buy, sizing, and whether to pass on a potential setup.

The information includes:

* News, Price Targets, Analyst Ratings
* RSI, Price
* Support and Resistance Levels
* Chart Formation Detection and Interpretation
* Rockkit Score which includes
   * Price Target
   * Mean Reversion
   * Analyst Rating
   * Price Velocity
   * Beta
   * Volume
   * Momentum - 3, 6, 12 Months performance %

All of the data we collect is persisted in the data base at the moment of the alert. The trajectory and outcome of every alert / trade is logged in the data base so the Stockkit system can perform statistical analysis on every alert and its outcome. The AI Trading Advisor has access to all of the data and can advise all of the traders who use the system based on their Tier: Free, Pro, Premium, Elite. The Elite Tier provides fully automated trading via Alpaca integration.

https://preview.redd.it/voxfu58wzj0h1.png?width=1956&format=png&auto=webp&s=3a050bd967cdc4e93a19f8e669ea45b3b2627615

Performing statistical analysis on "every stock" in the market is a huge task and the behavior is fairly random. Most stocks behave in similar ways and are affected by the same economic, financial, and geopolitical events. Buyers and sellers create oscillating patterns, but since every stock is affected by it's own earnings, news, analyst ratings, price targets, and emotions of the traders buying the stock, there's almost no way to detect patterns.

What the Stockkit system does is narrow the range of data to a very specific set. All the stocks are alerted on the same conditions. This allows for much better statistical analysis and how the system can achieve win rates of over 90%.

comments.author: CautiousPastrami
comments.body: Where do you source the data from?

replies.author: jabberw0ckee
replies.body: The system subscribes to several api end points for candles (OHLC) data, news, analyst ratings, and price targets, VIX feed, major index feed. They include: SEC, AlphaVantage, FMP, and Indices-api.

replies.author: CautiousPastrami
replies.body: I’m looking for some good APIs to fetch this type of data. What are your costs for connecting to those? How is rate limiting?

replies.author: ThickNick97
replies.body: I don’t know the comparison to other api’s, I’m just getting started with an ai of my own but I’m using alpaca.markets api for the live stock data, it’s free. If it ends up being subpar I might pay for one in the future

replies.author: jabberw0ckee
replies.body:
* AlphaVantage - Real Time Candles and Historical - Analyst Ratings and Price Targets - **$99** \- Second Tier - 150 API requests / min.
* Indices-api - VIX Real Time and Historical - **$99 -** 150 API requests / min.
* FMP Financial Modeling Prep - News / News Sentiment - **$29.00** \- 300 API requests / min.
* SEC - Full list of all equities (also provides information to filter out SPAC's, Warrants, ETF's, etc) - **Free**

replies.author: getstonkzilla
replies.body:
I’d recommend tiingo - $30 per month + $9 if you want fundamental data.

Rishi (ceo) is damn quick to reply for support. Smallish company keen to help customers.

replies.author: WittilyFun
replies.body:
We (Tiingo) also white label our data to several large APIs, been around 12 years, profitable, never raised money, and power both high schoolers and two of the largest brokerages in the business. So gives us freedom to build cool stuff, keep prices low to help small businesses/retail, and time for me to answer support emails and commit to the codebase 😄

Thanks for the kind words

comments.author: ksthd
comments.body: I stopped trying to 'guess' outcomes and started using the MiroFish engine to benchmark historical odds. The math is way more reliable than vibes: [**https://polymgloss.org**](https://polymgloss.org/)

replies.author: jabberw0ckee
replies.body: The smart thing about this algo + data + AI is that it doesn't "Guess" outcomes. Instead it reads the data, patterns, and price action of behavior that is already known. It tells you statistically which alerts have the highest probability of a positive outcome.

comments.author: paulet4a
comments.body:
Interesting approach — a few validation questions worth stress-testing before trusting those win rate numbers:

  
1. Are the 89%/84%/74% figures in-sample or out-of-sample? RSI-based signals look great in backtests on the same data they were tuned on. CPCV (combinatorial purged cross-validation) across multiple train/test path combinations usually reveals the real Sharpe distribution — often much wider than the point estimate.

  
2. What's the risk/reward ratio per trade? A 90% win rate with 0.15:1 R:R underperforms a 40% win rate at 3:1. Win rate alone without average win/loss ratio doesn't tell you if the system is profitable long-term.

  
3. Regime conditioning: RSI reversals work in ranging markets but tend to fail badly in strong trends. Did you break the win rates by regime (trending vs ranging)? If the 89% is all from ranging periods, it may invert in a trending environment.

replies.author: jabberw0ckee
replies.body:
1.	⁠The win rates are all forward tested. Real alerts, real time outcomes after the alert happened. Think about it, though: The stocks in our universes must achieve 55%, 65%, 75% and 85% for 3 month, 6 month, 12 month, and YTD. Many perform way better than that. SNDK, 3,000% in 12 months. WDC, 1,000% in 12 months. The best stocks create high win rates and high % wins.  
2.	⁠Each channel has its own stats. Ram Jet, as an example: 84% win rate. Average hold is 3 days. Average win is 3% (actual TP is recommended - 3%.) 10 day max hold. Average loss 6%. The system has a performance simulator that tracks the outcome of every trade - forward, not backtest. 3 lot system, Ram Jet has performed 718% since December 3rd 2025. It’s reliable and consistent taking only 3% gain and compounding massively 24 x 3% trades = 100% gain.  
3.	⁠The raw system performs well in all regimes. I’ve even backtested version on QC that select the high performers of the time from 2019 - present. There is drawdown and loss in bearish regimes but still profitable. I backtested over 100 different scenarios over 4 weekends to detect and mitigate bearish regimes. Conclusion: the methods to reduce loss in bearish regimes work to reduce loss in loss positions but also curtail wins of winning positions. Instead, what is successful: chart formation detection, support and resistance level detection and interpretation - are support levels being crossed? If so, don’t take the trade. Also statistical analysis - which trades (setup conditions) have the highest probability of winning - stick to these in bearish regimes. For example, alerts that occur between 9:30 - 10:00 EST and RSI dips to <25 has a 100% win rate and the average out come of the trade is 22% peak gain from the alert. These are stats from actual alerts - forward, not backtests.

replies.author: paulet4a
replies.body:
Forward testing is a meaningful distinction — that's real signal, not just curve-fitted backtest.

The universe filtering approach (stocks must hit 55/65/75/85% win thresholds across timeframes) is interesting — it's effectively a momentum/persistence screen baked into selection, which is a form of regime conditioning.

Two things I'd stress-test given the 718% since Dec 2025 figure:

1. That's ~5 months, almost entirely in a market recovery/risk-on environment. The real test is what happens when the regime flips hard — not just "bearish" but specifically low-liquidity, gap-heavy, high-correlation days where S/R levels get sliced through cleanly. Does the chart formation detection still identify valid setups, or does it fire on false breakouts?

2. On the "we backtested 100+ scenarios to handle bearish regimes" — what was the out-of-sample period? If the regime adjustment was tuned on historical bear markets, it may not generalize to the next bear market's specific character.

replies.author: jabberw0ckee
replies.body:
The Universe filtering creates lists like this - The Nitro Channel. 

1. I tested from 2019 to present using the top performers of the time period - similar results. Sector collapse was the biggest exposure. That actually happened just a few weeks ago with precious metals during Iran War pressure. Metals performed extremely well in the months prior and began to appear in the algo performance filter. I made the mistake of including too many metals stocks and when they collapsed at the end of April it was apparent. So, I'm creating sector collapse logic.   
  
2. I tested from 2019 to present though the decline in 2019, the pandemic and meltdown in 2022. The outcome was regime detection doesn't work. The raw strategy with no regime detection always out performed any kind of regime detection I threw at it. The regime detection did stop loss, but also curtailed profits. Much of this strategy capitalizes on the Momentum Effect by selecting the highest performing stocks, they are the stocks which bounce back the fastest after a sharp decline. Being in the best performers is a huge benefit. Since regime detection didn't work, I do not use regime detection so nothing is tuned to "historical bear markets."

Another "detector" I'm developing is an annual pattern decline detection. This is simply oversold RSI on the major indexes using 1 to 2 bars per day. This detects the annual dips that usually occur in November and March. The signal which tracks lower lows / lower highs  of RSI until it reaches RSI<30 is a successive warning over weeks and days to start unloading positions to be in cash $$ for a big downturn.

replies.author: paulet4a
replies.body:
the conclusion that regime detection curtailed wins more than it saved losses is a common finding for momentum-anchored strategies, lines up with what we see on the crypto side. the momentum effect is partly a survivorship signal - the names bouncing back hardest after a sharp drawdown are the same names you want to be in during the next leg up. binary regime-off filters cut both directions, which is why they bleed performance.

what usually works instead of a binary regime filter is size scaling. don't go to cash, just reduce position size when regime confidence drops. preserves the momentum exposure that drives the alpha but caps drawdown when environment turns. binary on/off is the wrong knob for momentum strategies, sizing is the right one.

the sector collapse logic is the more important guard imo. metals into Iran pressure is exactly the failure mode where pure momentum filtering doesn't help, because the universe filter selects what's been moving and that includes the over-extended names. correlation-based exposure capping per sector would catch that earlier than waiting for the collapse to register in price action.

the annual RSI pattern on SPX/NDX as a slow leader for individual stocks is a sane decoupling btw, the index regime tends to lead individual momentum names by 1-2 weeks at major turning points. should give you a quieter signal than trying to detect regime on the individual names themselves.

replies.author: jabberw0ckee
replies.body:
Reduced Position Sizing

This, too, was part of my back testing and it didn't seem to help. It could be that I didn't find the right combination. As the StockKit system creates more data, I should throw different scenarios at it including with different position sizing. The method that is beginning to emerge is specifically picking the stocks that are potentially bad trades and avoiding them. It's not about predicting though and instead pattern detection. The losing stocks illustrate a certain behavior which can be detected with a higher probability than not. 

Sector Collapse Logic and Negative Trends

The sector collapse is easily detected and trading other stocks is a better idea until the trend reveals itself. As for negative trends on stocks which have surged recently, StockKit also uses a negative trade filter and excludes any stocks with a negative performance for 3 months. Basically exclude any stocks with -1% or more decline in the past 3 months. 

Annual RSI Pattern on SPX

When RSI is tracked using 1 or 2 candles daily, enough noise is filtered out that the annual dip or two can be detected. However, the best way to play this is to start generating cash on the way down to the dip. So, it requires a way to start the alarm early - days or weeks - so traders can be cautious and more willingly cut trades earlier. What I've noticed is the RSI graph posts lower highs and lower lows on the way down. It can be up to 8 weeks of RSI oscillation posting lower lows and lower highs.

comments.author: misscoffeeaddict2
comments.body: ninety percent is a bold claim but i like the logic behind narrowing the data set to specific conditions. i have been using signalwhisper for a similar reason lately. it uses ai to calculate market intelligence and institutional-grade signals which really helps with that probability side of things. it is interesting to see more people focusing on the data science behind the trade rather than just guessing.

replies.author: jabberw0ckee
replies.body:
The Raw Alerts already have high win rates: 

* Nitro - 88.9%
* Ram Jet - 83.2%
* Rocket Fuel - 74%

With already high win rates, it doesn't take too much more to get them to above 90%. Some alerts have a 100% win rate. I perform statistical analysis on all the alerts. My most recent number crunch has some great insight. Here's a section of it that applies to the Free Tier for the Ram Jet Channel: 

Channel Correlation — Most Important Finding

The most predictive factor is whether the alerted stock appears in the **Rocket Fuel or Nitro universe.** 

Free Tier AI Trader Assistance Stats

* RSI < 20 Strong entry — RSI below 20 has a 93% historical TP rate. High conviction signal.
* **Symbol in Nitro universe Nitro-tracked stock — Ram Jet alerts on Nitro symbols have a 100% historical TP rate.**
* **Symbol in RF universe Rocket Fuel stock — RF symbols achieve 95.6% TP rate on Ram Jet alerts.**
* Morning alert Morning session. Valid signal (87% TP) but expect slightly lower avg gain than midday.
* Midday alert Best session. Midday alerts have the highest TP rate (91%) and avg PnL of any window.
* 1st-3rd alert Alert #{N} on this stock. 1st through 3rd alerts perform equally well (\~89% TP).
* 4th+ alert Caution: 4th+ consecutive alert. TP rate drops to 83% with lower avg PnL. Size smaller.
